channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Industry News
channel.description: Latest Industry News news in Context Engineering

item.title: RuleChef: Grounding LLM Task Knowledge in Human-Editable Rules
item.description: arXiv:2607.01293v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present RuleChef, a framework that uses large language models (LLMs) to generate executable rules for NLP tasks such as text classification, Named Entity Recognition (NER), or relation extraction. Rules are generated based on a task description and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, LLM, release, large language model, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Wiola Architecture for Efficient Small Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.01394v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present Wiola, a fully original Small Language Model (SLM) architecture built from first principles, sharing no structural lineage with any existing model family including GPT, LLaMA, Mistral, or Falcon. Wiola introduces five independently novel co...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, vector, attention, release, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Black-Box Inference of LLM Architectural Properties with Restrictive API Access
item.description: arXiv:2607.01313v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In practice, most commercial LLM providers do not publicly release details of underlying LLM architectures. However, prior work has shown that given limited API access to an LLM (namely, top-$k$ logits and/or a logit bias function), one can recover ce...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, LLM, vision, release, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: mcp-agent - Build effective agents using Model Context Protocol and simple workflow patterns
item.description: Build effective agents using Model Context Protocol and simple workflow patterns<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)
